testJsonEncode :: Assertion
testJsonEncode = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello'}"
                , "return json.encode(data)"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- The result should be a JSON string
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain x:1" $ "1" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

testJsonDecode :: Assertion
testJsonDecode = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local obj = json.decode('\"hello\"')"
                , "return obj"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello"

testJsonRoundtrip :: Assertion

        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello', z = true}"
                , "local encoded = json.encode(data)"
                , "local decoded = json.decode(encoded)"
                , "return decoded"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["x" .= (1 :: Int), "y" .= "hello", "z" .= True]

testJsonDecodeError :: Assertion

    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"

                , "return {ok = ok, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "ok" obj of
                        Just (Aeson.Bool b) -> b @?= False
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected ok=false"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Filesystem Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleFsTests :: TestTree
moduleFsTests =
    testGroup
        "Filesystem Module"
        [ testCase "fs.read reads file" testFsRead
        , testCase "fs.write writes file" testFsWrite
        , testCase "fs.exists checks existence" testFsExists
        , testCase "fs.isdir checks directory" testFsIsDir
        , testCase "fs.isfile checks file" testFsIsFile
        , testCase "fs.mkdir creates directory" testFsMkdir
        , testCase "fs.list lists directory" testFsList
        , testCase "fs sandbox blocks unauthorized path" testFsSandbox
        ]

testFsRead :: Assertion
testFsRead = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "test.txt") "hello world"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return fs.read('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "test.txt") <> "')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain hello" $ "hello" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

testFsWrite :: Assertion
testFsWrite = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local ok, err = fs.write('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "output.txt") <> "', 'test content')"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testFsExists :: Assertion
testFsExists = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "exists.txt") "content"
    result <-

            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {exists = fs.exists('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "exists.txt") <> "'), missing = fs.exists('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "missing.txt") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["exists" .= True, "missing" .= False]

testFsIsDir :: Assertion
testFsIsDir = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    createDirectoryIfMissing True (testDir </> "subdir")
    writeFile (testDir </> "file.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {dir = fs.isdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "subdir") <> "'), file = fs.isdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "file.txt") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["dir" .= True, "file" .= False]

testFsIsFile :: Assertion
testFsIsFile = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    createDirectoryIfMissing True (testDir </> "subdir")
    writeFile (testDir </> "file.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {file = fs.isfile('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "file.txt") <> "'), dir = fs.isfile('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "subdir") <> "')}"

                @?= Aeson.object ["file" .= True, "dir" .= False]

testFsMkdir :: Assertion
testFsMkdir = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local ok = fs.mkdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "newdir") <> "')"

    case result of

testFsList :: Assertion
testFsList = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "file1.txt") ""
    writeFile (testDir </> "file2.txt") ""
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local entries, err = fs.list('" <> Text.pack testDir <> "')"
                , "return #entries"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- Should have at least 2 files
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Number n -> assertBool "Should have at least 2 files" (n >= 2)
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected number"

testFsSandbox :: Assertion
testFsSandbox = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    -- Try to access a path outside the sandbox
    let unauthorizedPath = "/etc/passwd"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local content, err = fs.read('" <> Text.pack unauthorizedPath <> "')"
                , "return {content = content, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- Should return nil content with error
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "content" obj of
                        Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: content is nil
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil content"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Resource Limit Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

resourceLimitTests :: TestTree
resourceLimitTests =
    testGroup
        "Resource Limits"
        [ testCase "Timeout enforcement" testTimeout
        -- Note: Memory limit is difficult to test reliably in CI
        -- Skipping for now to avoid flakiness
        ]

testTimeout :: Assertion
testTimeout = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 1}
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "while true do end"
            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer box
            case result of
                Left (TimeoutError _) -> pure () -- Expected
                Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
                Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have timed out"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Error Handling Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

errorHandlingTests :: TestTree
errorHandlingTests =
    testGroup
        "Error Handling"
        [ testCase "Syntax error handling" testSyntaxError
        , testCase "Runtime error handling" testRuntimeError
        , testCase "Error handling with pcall" testPcallError
        ]

testSyntaxError :: Assertion
testSyntaxError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 1 +"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention syntax" $
                "syntax" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "expected" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with syntax error"

testRuntimeError :: Assertion
testRuntimeError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return nonexistent_variable"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with runtime error"

testPcallError :: Assertion
testPcallError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local ok, err = pcall(function() return nonexistent end)"
                , "return {success = ok, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "success" obj of
                        Just (Aeson.Bool b) -> b @?= False
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected success=false"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Portal Integration Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

portalIntegrationTests :: TestTree
portalIntegrationTests =
    testGroup
        "Portal Integration"
        [ testCase "Tool call with mock portal" testPortalToolCall
        , testCase "Tool call without portal fails gracefully" testNoPortal
        , testCase "Tool whitelist enforcement" testToolWhitelist
        ]

testPortalToolCall :: Assertion
testPortalToolCall = do
    -- Create a mock portal that echoes back the call
    let mockPortal call =
            pure $
                ToolResult
                    { resultData =
                        Aeson.object
                            [ "tool" .= callToolName call
                            , "args" .= callArgs call
                            ]
                    , resultDuration = 0.1
                    , resultTraceId = "test-trace-123"
                    }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["test_tool"]}

    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <-
                LuaToolbox.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    ( Text.unlines
                        [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                        , "local result = tools.call('test_tool', {x = 1})"
                        , "return result"
                        ]
                    )
                    (Just mockPortal)
                    ["test_tool"]
                    testNullTracerTools

            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer box

            case result of
                Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
                Right execResult -> do
                    case execResult.resultValue of
                        Aeson.Object resultObj -> do
                            case KeyMap.lookup "data" resultObj of
                                Just (Aeson.Object dataObj) -> do
                                    case KeyMap.lookup "tool" dataObj of
                                        Just (Aeson.String t) -> t @?= "test_tool"
                                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected tool name in data"
                                _ -> assertFailure "Expected data object"
                        _ -> assertFailure $ "Unexpected result: " ++ show execResult.resultValue

testNoPortal :: Assertion
testNoPortal = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    -- Try to call a tool without a portal
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                , "local result, err = tools.call('bash', {command = 'echo hello'})"
                , "return {result = result, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "result" obj of
                        Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: result is nil
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil result"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

testToolWhitelist :: Assertion
testToolWhitelist = do
    -- Create a mock portal that should not be called for blocked tool
    let mockPortal _ =
            pure $
                ToolResult
                    { resultData = Aeson.object ["result" .= "success"]
                    , resultDuration = 0.1
                    , resultTraceId = "test-trace"
                    }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["allowed_tool"]}

    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <-
                LuaToolbox.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    ( Text.unlines
                        [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                        , "local result, err = tools.call('blocked_tool', {})"
                        , "return {result = result, error = err}"
                        ]
                    )
                    (Just mockPortal)
                    ["allowed_tool"]
                    testNullTracerTools

            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer box

            case result of
                Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
                Right execResult ->
                    case execResult.resultValue of
                        Aeson.Object obj -> do
                            case KeyMap.lookup "result" obj of
                                Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: result is nil
                                _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil result for blocked tool"
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Helper Functions
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

testLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription
testLuaToolbox =
    LuaToolboxDescription
        { luaToolboxName = "test"
        , luaToolboxDescription = "Test toolbox"
        , luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 64
        , luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 10
        , luaToolboxAllowedTools = []
        , luaToolboxAllowedPaths = []
        , luaToolboxAllowedHosts = []
        }

-- | Null tracer that discards all trace events
testNullTracer :: Tracer IO LuaToolbox.Trace
testNullTracer = Tracer $ const $ pure ()

-- | Null tracer for Tools module (reuses the same type since Trace is the same)
testNullTracerTools :: Tracer IO LuaToolbox.Trace
testNullTracerTools = Tracer $ const $ pure ()

-- | Run an action with a test toolbox initialized
withTestToolbox :: (LuaToolbox.Toolbox -> IO ()) -> IO ()
withTestToolbox action = do
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer testLuaToolbox
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize toolbox: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            bracket
                (pure box)
                (\b -> LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer b)
                action

-- | Run an action with a test toolbox that has filesystem access
withTestToolboxFs :: (LuaToolbox.Toolbox -> FilePath -> IO ()) -> IO ()
withTestToolboxFs action = withTempSandbox $ \testDir -> do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedPaths = [testDir]}
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize toolbox: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            bracket
                (pure (box, testDir))
                (\(b, _) -> LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer b)
                (\(b, d) -> action b d)

-- | Create a temporary sandbox directory for testing
withTempSandbox :: (FilePath -> IO a) -> IO a
withTempSandbox action = do
    tmpDir <- createTempDirectory "/tmp" "luatoolbox-test-"
    bracket
        (pure tmpDir)
        removeDirectoryRecursive
        action
"}

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Basic Execution Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

basicExecutionTests :: TestTree
basicExecutionTests =
    testGroup
        "Basic Execution"
        [ testCase "Simple arithmetic" testSimpleArithmetic
        , testCase "Return table" testReturnTable
        , testCase "Return array" testReturnArray
        , testCase "Return string" testReturnString
        , testCase "Return boolean" testReturnBoolean
        , testCase "Return nil" testReturnNil
        ]

testSimpleArithmetic :: Assertion
testSimpleArithmetic = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 1 + 1"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 2

testReturnTable :: Assertion
testReturnTable = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return {foo = 'bar', count = 42}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["foo" .= "bar", "count" .= (42 :: Int)]

testReturnArray :: Assertion
testReturnArray = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return {1, 2, 3}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList [Aeson.Number 1, Aeson.Number 2, Aeson.Number 3])

testReturnString :: Assertion
testReturnString = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 'hello world'"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello world"

testReturnBoolean :: Assertion
testReturnBoolean = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return true"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testReturnNil :: Assertion
testReturnNil = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return nil"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Null

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Sandbox Security Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

sandboxSecurityTests :: TestTree
sandboxSecurityTests =
    testGroup
        "Sandbox Security"
        [ testCase "Sandbox blocks os.execute" testSandboxBlocksOsExecute
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.popen" testSandboxBlocksIoPopen
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks dofile" testSandboxBlocksDofile
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks loadfile" testSandboxBlocksLoadfile
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.remove" testSandboxBlocksOsRemove
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.rename" testSandboxBlocksOsRename
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.exit" testSandboxBlocksOsExit
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.tmpfile" testSandboxBlocksIoTmpfile
        ]

testSandboxBlocksOsExecute :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsExecute = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.execute('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksIoPopen :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksIoPopen = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "io.popen('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- JSON Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleJsonTests :: TestTree
moduleJsonTests =
    testGroup
        "JSON Module"
        [ testCase "JSON encode" testJsonEncode
        , testCase "JSON decode" testJsonDecode
        , testCase "JSON roundtrip" testJsonRoundtrip
        , testCase "JSON decode error handling" testJsonDecodeError
        ]

testJsonEncode :: Assertion
testJsonEncode = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello'}"
                , "return json.encode(data)"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- The result should be a JSON string
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain x:1" $ "1" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

testJsonDecode :: Assertion
testJsonDecode = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local obj = json.decode('\"hello\"')"
                , "return obj"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello"

testJsonRoundtrip :: Assertion
testJsonRoundtrip = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello', z = true}"
                , "local encoded = json.encode(data)"
                , "local decoded = json.decode(encoded)"
                , "return decoded"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["x" .= (1 :: Int), "y" .= "hello", "z" .= True]

testJsonDecodeError :: Assertion
testJsonDecodeError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local ok, err = pcall(function() json.decode('invalid json') end)"
                , "return {ok = ok, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "ok" obj of
                        Just (Aeson.Bool b) -> b @?= False
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected ok=false"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Time Module Tests

moduleTimeTests :: TestTree
moduleTimeTests =
    testGroup
        "Time Module"
        [ testCase "time.now returns timestamp" testTimeNow
        , testCase "time.sleep works" testTimeSleep
        , testCase "time.diff calculates difference" testTimeDiff
        , testCase "time.format formats timestamp" testTimeFormat
        ]

testTimeNow :: Assertion
testTimeNow = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local now = time.now()"
                , "return now"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Number n ->
                    -- Should be a reasonable timestamp (after 2020)
                    assertBool "Timestamp should be positive" (n > 0)
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected number (timestamp)"

testTimeSleep :: Assertion
testTimeSleep = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    -- Sleep for a very short time (0.01 seconds)
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local ok = time.sleep(0.01)"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testTimeDiff :: Assertion
testTimeDiff = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local diff = time.diff(100, 50)"
                , "return diff"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 50

testTimeFormat :: Assertion
testTimeFormat = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "-- Format Unix epoch 0 (1970-01-01)"
                , "local formatted = time.format(0, '%Y-%m-%d')"
                , "return formatted"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain 1970" $ "1970" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Filesystem Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleFsTests :: TestTree
moduleFsTests =
    testGroup
        "Filesystem Module"
        [ testCase "fs.read reads file" testFsRead
        , testCase "fs.write writes file" testFsWrite
        , testCase "fs.exists checks existence" testFsExists
        , testCase "fs.isdir checks directory" testFsIsDir
        , testCase "fs.isfile checks file" testFsIsFile
        , testCase "fs.mkdir creates directory" testFsMkdir
        , testCase "fs.list lists directory" testFsList
        , testCase "fs sandbox blocks unauthorized path" testFsSandbox
        ]

testFsRead :: Assertion
testFsRead = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "test.txt") "hello world"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return fs.read('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "test.txt") <> "')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain hello" $ "hello" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

testFsWrite :: Assertion
testFsWrite = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local ok, err = fs.write('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "output.txt") <> "', 'test content')"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testFsExists :: Assertion
testFsExists = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "exists.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {exists = fs.exists('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "exists.txt") <> "'), missing = fs.exists('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "missing.txt") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["exists" .= True, "missing" .= False]

testFsIsDir :: Assertion
testFsIsDir = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    createDirectoryIfMissing True (testDir </> "subdir")
    writeFile (testDir </> "file.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {dir = fs.isdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "subdir") <> "'), file = fs.isdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "file.txt") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["dir" .= True, "file" .= False]

testFsIsFile :: Assertion
testFsIsFile = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    createDirectoryIfMissing True (testDir </> "subdir")
    writeFile (testDir </> "file.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {file = fs.isfile('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "file.txt") <> "'), dir = fs.isfile('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "subdir") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["file" .= True, "dir" .= False]

testFsMkdir :: Assertion
testFsMkdir = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local ok = fs.mkdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "newdir") <> "')"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testFsList :: Assertion
testFsList = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "file1.txt") ""
    writeFile (testDir </> "file2.txt") ""
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local entries, err = fs.list('" <> Text.pack testDir <> "')"
                , "return #entries"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- Should have at least 2 files
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Number n -> assertBool "Should have at least 2 files" (n >= 2)
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected number"

testFsSandbox :: Assertion
testFsSandbox = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    -- Try to access a path outside the sandbox
    let unauthorizedPath = "/etc/passwd"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local content, err = fs.read('" <> Text.pack unauthorizedPath <> "')"
                , "return {content = content, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- Should return nil content with error
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "content" obj of
                        Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: content is nil
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil content"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Resource Limit Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

resourceLimitTests :: TestTree
resourceLimitTests =
    testGroup
        "Resource Limits"
        [ testCase "Timeout enforcement" testTimeout
        -- Note: Memory limit is difficult to test reliably in CI
        -- Skipping for now to avoid flakiness
        ]

testTimeout :: Assertion
testTimeout = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 1}
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "while true do end"
            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer box
            case result of
                Left (TimeoutError _) -> pure () -- Expected
                Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
                Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have timed out"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Error Handling Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

errorHandlingTests :: TestTree
errorHandlingTests =
    testGroup
        "Error Handling"
        [ testCase "Syntax error handling" testSyntaxError
        , testCase "Runtime error handling" testRuntimeError
        , testCase "Error handling with pcall" testPcallError
        ]

testSyntaxError :: Assertion
testSyntaxError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 1 +"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention syntax" $
                "syntax" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "expected" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with syntax error"

testRuntimeError :: Assertion
testRuntimeError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return nonexistent_variable"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with runtime error"

testPcallError :: Assertion
testPcallError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local ok, err = pcall(function() return nonexistent end)"
                , "return {success = ok, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "success" obj of
                        Just (Aeson.Bool b) -> b @?= False
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected success=false"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

portalIntegrationTests :: TestTree
portalIntegrationTests =
    testGroup
        "Portal Integration"
        [ testCase "Tool call with mock portal" testPortalToolCall
        , testCase "Tool call without portal fails gracefully" testNoPortal
        , testCase "Tool whitelist enforcement" testToolWhitelist
        ]

testPortalToolCall :: Assertion
testPortalToolCall = do
    -- Create a mock portal that echoes back the call
    let mockPortal call =
            pure $
                ToolResult
                    { resultData =
                        Aeson.object
                            [ "tool" .= callToolName call
                            , "args" .= callArgs call
                            ]
                    , resultDuration = 0.1
                    , resultTraceId = "test-trace-123"
                    }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["test_tool"]}

    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <-
                LuaToolbox.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    ( Text.unlines
                        [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                        , "local result = tools.call('test_tool', {x = 1})"
                        , "return result"
                        ]
                    )
                    (Just mockPortal)
                    ["test_tool"]
                    testNullTracerTools

            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer box

            case result of
                Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
                Right execResult -> do
                    case execResult.resultValue of
                        Aeson.Object resultObj -> do
                            case KeyMap.lookup "data" resultObj of
                                Just (Aeson.Object dataObj) -> do
                                    case KeyMap.lookup "tool" dataObj of
                                        Just (Aeson.String t) -> t @?= "test_tool"
                                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected tool name in data"
                                _ -> assertFailure "Expected data object"
                        _ -> assertFailure $ "Unexpected result: " ++ show execResult.resultValue

testNoPortal :: Assertion
testNoPortal = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    -- Try to call a tool without a portal
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                , "local result, err = tools.call('bash', {command = 'echo hello'})"
                , "return {result = result, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "result" obj of
                        Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: result is nil
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil result"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

testToolWhitelist :: Assertion
testToolWhitelist = do
    -- Create a mock portal that should not be called for blocked tool
    let mockPortal _ =
            pure $
                ToolResult
                    { resultData = Aeson.object ["result" .= "success"]
                    , resultDuration = 0.1
                    , resultTraceId = "test-trace"
                    }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["allowed_tool"]}

    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <-
                LuaToolbox.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    ( Text.unlines
                        [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                        , "local result, err = tools.call('blocked_tool', {})"
                        , "return {result = result, error = err}"
                        ]
                    )
                    (Just mockPortal)
                    ["allowed_tool"]
                    testNullTracerTools

            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer box

            case result of
                Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
                Right execResult ->
                    case execResult.resultValue of
                        Aeson.Object obj -> do
                            case KeyMap.lookup "result" obj of
                                Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: result is nil
                                _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil result for blocked tool"
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Helper Functions
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

testLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription
testLuaToolbox =
    LuaToolboxDescription
        { luaToolboxName = "test"
        , luaToolboxDescription = "Test toolbox"
        , luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 64
        , luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 10
        , luaToolboxAllowedTools = []
        , luaToolboxAllowedPaths = []
        , luaToolboxAllowedHosts = []
        }

-- | Null tracer that discards all trace events
testNullTracer :: Tracer IO LuaToolbox.Trace
testNullTracer = Tracer $ const $ pure ()

-- | Null tracer for Tools module (reuses the same type since Trace is the same)
testNullTracerTools :: Tracer IO LuaToolbox.Trace
testNullTracerTools = Tracer $ const $ pure ()

-- | Run an action with a test toolbox initialized
withTestToolbox :: (LuaToolbox.Toolbox -> IO ()) -> IO ()
withTestToolbox action = do
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer testLuaToolbox
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize toolbox: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            bracket
                (pure box)
                (\b -> LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer b)
                action

-- | Run an action with a test toolbox that has filesystem access
withTestToolboxFs :: (LuaToolbox.Toolbox -> FilePath -> IO ()) -> IO ()
withTestToolboxFs action = withTempSandbox $ \testDir -> do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedPaths = [testDir]}
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox testNullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize toolbox: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            bracket
                (pure (box, testDir))
                (\(b, _) -> LuaToolbox.closeToolbox testNullTracer b)
                (\(b, d) -> action b d)

-- | Create a temporary sandbox directory for testing
withTempSandbox :: (FilePath -> IO a) -> IO a
withTempSandbox action = do
    tmpDir <- createTempDirectory "/tmp" "luatoolbox-test-"
    bracket
        (pure tmpDir)
        removeDirectoryRecursive
        action
"}

-- The cabal-version field refers to the version of the .cabal specification,
-- and can be different from the cabal-install (the tool) version and the
-- Cabal (the library) version you are using. As such, the Cabal (the library)
-- version used must be equal or greater than the version stated in this field.
-- Starting from the specification version 2.2, the cabal-version field must be
-- the first thing in the .cabal specification.

-- Initial package description 'agents-exe' generated by
-- 'cabal init'. For further documentation, see:
--   http://haskell.org/cabal/users-guide/
--
-- The name of the package.
name:               agents

-- The package version.
-- See the package version versioning policy (PVP) for standards
-- guiding when and how versions should be incremented.
-- https://pvp.haskell.org/
-- PVP summary:     +-+------- breaking API changes
--                  | | +----- non-breaking API additions
--                  | | | +--- code changes with no API change
version:            0.1.0.0

-- A short (one-line) description of the package.
-- synopsis:

-- A longer description of the package.
-- description:

-- The license under which the package is released.
license:            Apache-2.0

-- The file containing the license text.
license-file:      LICENSE

-- The package author(s).
author:             Lucas DiCioccio

-- An email address to which users can send suggestions, bug reports, and patches.
maintainer:         lucas@dicioccio.fr

-- A copyright notice.
-- copyright:
category:           System
build-type:         Simple

-- Extra doc files to be distributed with the package, such as a CHANGELOG or a tutorial.
extra-doc-files:    CHANGELOG.md

-- Extra source files to be distributed with the package, such as examples, or a tutorial.
-- extra-source-files:

common warnings
    ghc-options: -Wall

library agents-lib
    import:           warnings
    hs-source-dirs:   src
    default-language: Haskell2010
    default-extensions: OverloadedStrings, OverloadedRecordDot
    exposed-modules:  System.Agents
                    , System.Agents.AgentTree
                    , System.Agents.AgentTree.OneShotTool
                    , System.Agents.ApiKeys
                    , System.Agents.Base
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Aliases
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Base
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Check
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Cowsay
                    , System.Agents.CLI.EchoPrompt
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Export
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Import
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Initialize
                    , System.Agents.CLI.InitProject
                    , System.Agents.CLI.McpServer
                    , System.Agents.CLI.OneShot
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Paths
                    , System.Agents.CLI.PromptScript
                    , System.Agents.CLI.SelfDescribe
                    , System.Agents.CLI.SessionEdit
                    , System.Agents.CLI.TraceUtils
                    , System.Agents.CLI.TUI
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.Types
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.Namespace
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.Archive
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.Git
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.ToolInstall
                    , System.Agents.FileLoader
                    , System.Agents.FileLoader.JSON
                    , System.Agents.FileNotification
                    , System.Agents.ToolRegistration
                    , System.Agents.Tools
                    , System.Agents.Tools.Context
                    , System.Agents.Tools.Trace
                    , System.Agents.Tools.Base
                    , System.Agents.Tools.Bash
                    , System.Agents.Tools.BashToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.EndpointPredicate
                    , System.Agents.Tools.IO
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Text
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Time
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Fs
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Http
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Tools
                    , System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types
                    , System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Resolver
                    , System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter
                    , System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.PostgREST.Types
                    , System.Agents.Tools.PostgREST.Converter
                    , System.Agents.Tools.PostgRESToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.SqliteToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.SystemToolbox
                    , System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI
                    , System.Agents.Runtime
                    , System.Agents.Runtime.Runtime
                    , System.Agents.Runtime.Trace
                    , System.Agents.Session.Types
                    , System.Agents.Session.Base
                    , System.Agents.Session.Edit
                    , System.Agents.Session.OpenAI
                    , System.Agents.Session.Loop
                    , System.Agents.Session.Step
                    , System.Agents.SessionPrint
                    , System.Agents.SessionPrint.Inject
                    , System.Agents.SessionStore
                    , System.Agents.ToolSchema
                    , System.Agents.HttpClient
                    , System.Agents.HttpLogger
                    , System.Agents.OneShot
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Base
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Client
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Client.Runtime
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Server
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Server.Runtime
                    , System.Agents.TUI.Core
                    , System.Agents.TUI.Types
                    , System.Agents.TUI.Render
                    , System.Agents.TUI.Event
                    , System.Agents.TraceUtils
    build-depends:    base  >=4.18.3.0,
                      aeson >=2.2.0.0,
                      aeson-pretty,
                      async,
                      base64-bytestring,
                      brick,
                      bytestring,
                      case-insensitive,
                      conduit,
                      conduit-extra,
                      containers,
                      contravariant,
                      data-clist,
                      direct-sqlite,
                      directory,
                      extra,
                      filepath,
                      formatting,
                      free,
                      haskeline,
                      hslua >= 2.3,
                      hslua-marshalling,
                      http-client,
                      http-client-tls,
                      http-types,
                      indexed-traversable,
                      json-rpc,
                      lens,
                      monad-logger,
                      mtl,
                      network-uri,
                      process,
                      process-extras,
                      prodapi,
                      fsnotify,
                      scientific,
                      sqlite-simple,
                      stm,
                      stm-chans,
                      stm-conduit,
                      tar,
                      temporary,
                      text,
                      text-zipper,
                      time,
                      unix,
                      unliftio,
                      unordered-containers,
                      uuid,
                      vector,
                      vty,
                      zlib
                      

executable agents-exe
    -- Import common warning flags.
    import:           warnings

    -- .hs or .hs file containing the Main module.
    main-is:          Main.hs
    ghc-options: -threaded

    -- Modules included in the executable, other than Main.
    -- other-modules:

    -- LANGUAGE extensions used by modules in the executable.
    -- other-extensions:

    -- Other library packages from which modules are imported.
    build-depends:    base  >=4.18.3.0,
                      agents-lib,
                      aeson,
                      aeson-pretty,
                      bytestring,
                      containers,
                      contravariant,
                      directory,
                      filepath,
                      text,
                      process,
                      prodapi,
                      optparse-applicative,
                      time,
                      temporary,
                      unix

    -- Directories containing the source files.
    hs-source-dirs:   app

    -- Base language which the package written in.
    default-language: Haskell2010

executable agq
    import:           warnings
    main-is:          Main.hs
    ghc-options:      -threaded
    other-modules:    Agq.Config
                    , Agq.DB
                    , Agq.Schedule
                    , Agq.Run
                    , Agq.Commands
    build-depends:    base >=4.18.3.0,
                      aeson,
                      aeson-pretty,
                      async,
                      bytestring,
                      containers,
                      directory,
                      filepath,
                      optparse-applicative,
                      process,
                      scientific,
                      sqlite-simple,
                      text,
                      time,
                      unix
    hs-source-dirs:   agq
    default-language: Haskell2010
    default-extensions: OverloadedStrings, OverloadedRecordDot

test-suite agents-tests
    type: exitcode-stdio-1.0
    hs-source-dirs: test
    main-is: Main.hs
    other-modules: OpenAPIToolboxTests
                   PostgRESTParseTest
                   SessionEditTests
                   SessionPrintTests
                   EndpointPredicateTests
                   LuaToolboxSecurityTests
                   LuaToolboxTests
    default-language: Haskell2010
    default-extensions: OverloadedStrings, OverloadedRecordDot
    build-depends:    base >=4.18.3.0,
                      agents-lib,
                      aeson,
                      bytestring,
                      containers,
                      directory,
                      filepath,
                      prodapi,
                      text,
                      tasty,
                      tasty-hunit,
                      temporary
"}

function.name: bash_write-file

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

category:           System
build-type:         Simple

-- Extra doc files to be distributed with the package, such as a CHANGELOG or a tutorial.
extra-doc-files:    CHANGELOG.md

-- Extra source files to be distributed with the package, such as examples, or a tutorial.
-- extra-source-files:

common warnings
    ghc-options: -Wall

library agents-lib
    import:           warnings
    hs-source-dirs:   src
    default-language: Haskell2010
    default-extensions: OverloadedStrings, OverloadedRecordDot
    exposed-modules:  System.Agents
                    , System.Agents.AgentTree
                    , System.Agents.AgentTree.OneShotTool
                    , System.Agents.ApiKeys
                    , System.Agents.Base
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Aliases
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Base
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Check
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Cowsay
                    , System.Agents.CLI.EchoPrompt
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Export
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Import
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Initialize
                    , System.Agents.CLI.InitProject
                    , System.Agents.CLI.McpServer
                    , System.Agents.CLI.OneShot
                    , System.Agents.CLI.Paths
                    , System.Agents.CLI.PromptScript
                    , System.Agents.CLI.SelfDescribe
                    , System.Agents.CLI.SessionEdit
                    , System.Agents.CLI.TraceUtils
                    , System.Agents.CLI.TUI
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.Types
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.Namespace
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.Archive
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.Git
                    , System.Agents.ExportImport.ToolInstall
                    , System.Agents.FileLoader
                    , System.Agents.FileLoader.JSON
                    , System.Agents.FileNotification
                    , System.Agents.ToolRegistration
                    , System.Agents.Tools
                    , System.Agents.Tools.Context
                    , System.Agents.Tools.Trace
                    , System.Agents.Tools.Base
                    , System.Agents.Tools.Bash
                    , System.Agents.Tools.BashToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.EndpointPredicate
                    , System.Agents.Tools.IO
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Text
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Time
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Fs
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Http
                    , System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Tools
                    , System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types
                    , System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Resolver
                    , System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter
                    , System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.PostgREST.Types
                    , System.Agents.Tools.PostgREST.Converter
                    , System.Agents.Tools.PostgRESToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.SqliteToolbox
                    , System.Agents.Tools.SystemToolbox
                    , System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI
                    , System.Agents.Runtime
                    , System.Agents.Runtime.Runtime
                    , System.Agents.Runtime.Trace
                    , System.Agents.Session.Types
                    , System.Agents.Session.Base
                    , System.Agents.Session.Edit
                    , System.Agents.Session.OpenAI
                    , System.Agents.Session.Loop
                    , System.Agents.Session.Step
                    , System.Agents.SessionPrint
                    , System.Agents.SessionPrint.Inject
                    , System.Agents.SessionStore
                    , System.Agents.ToolSchema
                    , System.Agents.HttpClient
                    , System.Agents.HttpLogger
                    , System.Agents.OneShot
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Base
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Client
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Client.Runtime
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Server
                    , System.Agents.MCP.Server.Runtime
                    , System.Agents.TUI.Core
                    , System.Agents.TUI.Types
                    , System.Agents.TUI.Render
                    , System.Agents.TUI.Event
                    , System.Agents.TraceUtils
    build-depends:    base  >=4.18.3.0,
                      aeson >=2.2.0.0,
                      aeson-pretty,
                      async,
                      base64-bytestring,
                      brick,
                      bytestring,
                      case-insensitive,
                      conduit,
                      conduit-extra,
                      containers,
                      contravariant,
                      data-clist,
                      direct-sqlite,
                      directory,
                      extra,
                      filepath,
                      formatting,
                      free,
                      haskeline,
                      hslua >= 2.3,
                      hslua-marshalling,
                      http-client,
                      http-client-tls,
                      http-types,
                      indexed-traversable,
                      json-rpc,
                      lens,
                      monad-logger,
                      mtl,
                      network-uri,
                      process,
                      process-extras,
                      prodapi,
                      fsnotify,
                      scientific,
                      sqlite-simple,
                      stm,
                      stm-chans,
                      stm-conduit,
                      tar,
                      temporary,
                      text,
                      text-zipper,
                      time,
                      unix,
                      unliftio,
                      unordered-containers,
                      uuid,
                      vector,
                      vty,
                      zlib
                      

executable agents-exe
    -- Import common warning flags.
    import:           warnings

    -- .hs or .hs file containing the Main module.
    main-is:          Main.hs
    ghc-options: -threaded

    -- Modules included in the executable, other than Main.
    -- other-modules:

    -- LANGUAGE extensions used by modules in the executable.
    -- other-extensions:

    -- Other library packages from which modules are imported.
    build-depends:    base  >=4.18.3.0,
                      agents-lib,
                      aeson,
                      aeson-pretty,
                      bytestring,
                      containers,
                      contravariant,
                      directory,
                      filepath,
                      text,
                      process,
                      prodapi,
                      optparse-applicative,
                      time,
                      temporary,
                      unix

    -- Directories containing the source files.
    hs-source-dirs:   app

    -- Base language which the package written in.
    default-language: Haskell2010

executable agq
    import:           warnings
    main-is:          Main.hs
    ghc-options:      -threaded
    other-modules:    Agq.Config
                    , Agq.DB
                    , Agq.Schedule
                    , Agq.Run
                    , Agq.Commands
    build-depends:    base >=4.18.3.0,
                      aeson,
                      aeson-pretty,
                      async,
                      bytestring,
                      containers,
                      directory,
                      filepath,
                      optparse-applicative,
                      process,
                      scientific,
                      sqlite-simple,
                      text,
                      time,
                      unix
    hs-source-dirs:   agq
    default-language: Haskell2010
    default-extensions: OverloadedStrings, OverloadedRecordDot

test-suite agents-tests
    type: exitcode-stdio-1.0
    hs-source-dirs: test
    main-is: Main.hs
    other-modules: OpenAPIToolboxTests
                   PostgRESTParseTest
                   SessionEditTests
                   SessionPrintTests
                   EndpointPredicateTests
                   LuaToolboxSecurityTests
                   LuaToolboxTests
    default-language: Haskell2010
    default-extensions: OverloadedStrings, OverloadedRecordDot
    build-depends:    base >=4.18.3.0,
                      agents-lib,
                      aeson,
                      bytestring,
                      containers,
                      directory,
                      filepath,
                      text,
                      tasty,
                      tasty-hunit,
                      temporary
"}

                    , Agq.Commands

                      aeson,

{"filepath": "./test/Main.hs", "content": "module Main where

import System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI hiding (SystemPrompt)

import System.Agents.Tools.Context as Context
import qualified System.Agents.Session.Types as SessionTypes

import System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types as OpenAPI hiding (object, (.=))
import System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Resolver as Resolver
import System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter as Converter

import qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LBS
import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map

import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import Test.Tasty
import Test.Tasty.HUnit

-- Import PostgREST parsing tests

-- Import Session Edit tests

-- Import LuaToolbox security tests

-- Import LuaToolbox comprehensive tests

        ]

openAIRateLimitTests :: TestTree
openAIRateLimitTests =

        "OpenAI rate-limits"
        [ testCase "1d1h1m1s" $
            parseRateLimitDelay "1d1h1m1s" @?= 1 + 60 + 3600 + 86400
        , testCase "1h1m1s" $
            parseRateLimitDelay "1h1m1s" @?= 1 + 60 + 3600
        , testCase "1m1s" $

        , testCase "1.376s" $

        , testCase "120ms" $

        ]

extraAgentRefTests :: TestTree
extraAgentRefTests =
    testGroup
        "ExtraAgentRef JSON serialization"
        [ testCase "serialize to JSON" $ do
            let ref = Base.ExtraAgentRef { Base.extraAgentSlug = "helper-bot", Base.extraAgentPath = "./helpers/bot.json" }
            let json = encode ref
            Text.unpack (decodeUtf8 json) @?= "{\"slug\":\"helper-bot\",\"path\":\"./helpers/bot.json\"}"
        , testCase "deserialize from JSON" $ do
            let json = "{\"slug\":\"helper-bot\",\"path\":\"./helpers/bot.json\"}"
            let mRef = decode (encodeUtf8 json) :: Maybe Base.ExtraAgentRef
            mRef @?= Just (Base.ExtraAgentRef { Base.extraAgentSlug = "helper-bot", Base.extraAgentPath = "./helpers/bot.json" })
        , testCase "round-trip" $ do
            let ref = Base.ExtraAgentRef { Base.extraAgentSlug = "superb-agent", Base.extraAgentPath = "../superb/agent.json" }
            let json = encode ref
            let mRef = decode json :: Maybe Base.ExtraAgentRef

        ]
  where
    encodeUtf8 = LBS.fromStrict . Text.encodeUtf8
    decodeUtf8 = Text.decodeUtf8 . LBS.toStrict

agentSerializationTests :: TestTree
agentSerializationTests =
    testGroup
        "Agent JSON serialization"
        [ testCase "backward compatibility - agent without extra-agents" $ do
            -- This JSON format should still work with existing agent.json files
            let json = Text.unlines
                    [ "{"
                    , "  \"slug\": \"test-agent\","
                    , "  \"apiKeyId\": \"openai\","
                    , "  \"flavor\": \"openai\","

                    , "  \"systemPrompt\": [\"You are helpful\"],"
                    , "  \"toolDirectory\": \"tools\""
                    , "}"
                    ]
            let mAgent = decode (encodeUtf8 json) :: Maybe Base.Agent
            case mAgent of
                Nothing -> assertFailure "Failed to parse agent without extra-agents"
                Just agent -> do
                    Base.slug agent @?= "test-agent"
                    Base.extraAgents agent @?= Nothing

        , testCase "agent with extra-agents" $ do

                    , "}"
                    ]
            let mAgent = decode (encodeUtf8 json) :: Maybe Base.Agent
            case mAgent of
                Nothing -> assertFailure "Failed to parse agent with extra-agents"
                Just agent -> do
                    Base.slug agent @?= "boss-kimi"

        , testCase "round-trip with extra-agents" $ do
            let agent = Base.Agent

                    , Base.modelName = "gpt-4"

                        ]
                    }
            let json = encode agent
            let mAgent = decode json :: Maybe Base.Agent
            mAgent @?= Just agent
        , testCase "round-trip without extra-agents" $ do
            let agent = Base.Agent
                    { Base.slug = "test-agent"
                    , Base.apiKeyId = "openai"
                    , Base.flavor = "openai"
                    , Base.modelUrl = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
                    , Base.modelName = "gpt-4"
                    , Base.announce = "A test agent"
                    , Base.systemPrompt = ["You are helpful"]
                    , Base.toolDirectory = "tools"
                    , Base.mcpServers = Nothing
                    , Base.openApiToolboxes = Nothing
                    , Base.postgrestToolboxes = Nothing
                    , Base.builtinToolboxes = Nothing
                    , Base.extraAgents = Nothing
                    }
            let json = encode agent
            let mAgent = decode json :: Maybe Base.Agent
            mAgent @?= Just agent
        , testCase "AgentDescription round-trip" $ do
            let agent = Base.Agent
                    { Base.slug = "test-agent"
                    , Base.apiKeyId = "openai"
                    , Base.flavor = "openai"
                    , Base.modelUrl = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
                    , Base.modelName = "gpt-4"
                    , Base.announce = "A test agent"
                    , Base.systemPrompt = ["You are helpful"]
                    , Base.toolDirectory = "tools"
                    , Base.mcpServers = Just []
                    , Base.openApiToolboxes = Nothing
                    , Base.postgrestToolboxes = Nothing
                    , Base.builtinToolboxes = Nothing
                    , Base.extraAgents = Just
                        [ Base.ExtraAgentRef { Base.extraAgentSlug = "helper", Base.extraAgentPath = "./helper.json" }
                        ]
                    }
            let desc = Base.AgentDescription agent
            let json = encode desc
            let mDesc = decode json :: Maybe Base.AgentDescription
            mDesc @?= Just desc
        , testCase "round-trip with builtin toolboxes" $ do
            let sqliteDesc = Base.SqliteToolboxDescription
                    { Base.sqliteToolboxName = "memory"
                    , Base.sqliteToolboxDescription = "a set of memories"
                    , Base.sqliteToolboxPath = "/path/to/memories.sqlite"
                    , Base.sqliteToolboxAccess = Base.SqliteReadWrite
                    }
            let builtinToolbox = Base.SqliteToolbox sqliteDesc
            let agent = Base.Agent
                    { Base.slug = "test-agent"
                    , Base.apiKeyId = "openai"
                    , Base.flavor = "openai"
                    , Base.modelUrl = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
                    , Base.modelName = "gpt-4"
                    , Base.announce = "A test agent"

                    }
            let json = encode agent
            let mAgent = decode json :: Maybe Base.Agent
            mAgent @?= Just agent
        , testCase "round-trip with multiple builtin toolboxes" $ do
            let sqliteDesc1 = Base.SqliteToolboxDescription
                    { Base.sqliteToolboxName = "memory"
                    , Base.sqliteToolboxDescription = "a set of memories"

            let sqliteDesc2 = Base.SqliteToolboxDescription
                    { Base.sqliteToolboxName = "guidelines"
                    , Base.sqliteToolboxDescription = "a set of guidelines"
                    , Base.sqliteToolboxPath = "/path/to/guidelines.sqlite"

            let builtinToolboxes = [Base.SqliteToolbox sqliteDesc1, Base.SqliteToolbox sqliteDesc2]

        , testCase "SqliteAccessMode round-trip" $ do

            -- Test read-write mode round-trip
            let readWrite = Base.SqliteReadWrite
            let jsonRW = encode readWrite
            let mReadWrite = decode jsonRW :: Maybe Base.SqliteAccessMode
            mReadWrite @?= Just Base.SqliteReadWrite
        , testCase "round-trip with LuaToolbox" $ do
            let luaDesc = Base.LuaToolboxDescription

                    , Base.modelName = "gpt-4"
                    , Base.announce = "A test agent"
                    , Base.systemPrompt = ["You are helpful"]
                    , Base.toolDirectory = "tools"
                    , Base.mcpServers = Nothing
                    , Base.openApiToolboxes = Nothing
                    , Base.postgrestToolboxes = Nothing
                    , Base.builtinToolboxes = Just [builtinToolbox]
