{"filepath": "./test/LuaToolboxTests.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}

-- | Comprehensive unit tests for LuaToolbox.
--
-- These tests verify:
-- * Basic Lua execution (arithmetic, tables)
-- * Sandbox security (os.execute, io.popen blocked)
-- * Standard library modules (json, text, time)
-- * Resource limits (memory, timeout)
-- * Filesystem sandboxing
-- * HTTP sandboxing
-- * Error handling
module LuaToolboxTests where

import Control.Exception (bracket)
import Data.Aeson ((.=))
import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import System.Directory (createDirectoryIfMissing, removeDirectoryRecursive)
import System.FilePath ((</>))
import System.IO (writeFile)
import System.IO.Temp (createTempDirectory)
import Test.Tasty
import Test.Tasty.HUnit

import Prod.Tracer (Tracer (..))

import System.Agents.Base (LuaToolboxDescription (..))
import System.Agents.Tools.Context (ToolCall (..), ToolPortal, ToolResult (..))
import System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox as LuaToolbox

luaToolboxTests :: TestTree
luaToolboxTests =
    testGroup
        "LuaToolbox Tests"
        [ basicExecutionTests
        , sandboxSecurityTests
        , moduleJsonTests
        , moduleTextTests
        , moduleTimeTests
        , moduleFsTests
        , resourceLimitTests
        , errorHandlingTests
        , portalIntegrationTests
        ]

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Basic Execution Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

basicExecutionTests :: TestTree
basicExecutionTests =
    testGroup
        "Basic Execution"
        [ testCase "Simple arithmetic" testSimpleArithmetic
        , testCase "Return table" testReturnTable
        , testCase "Return array" testReturnArray
        , testCase "Return string" testReturnString
        , testCase "Return boolean" testReturnBoolean
        , testCase "Return nil" testReturnNil
        ]

testSimpleArithmetic :: Assertion
testSimpleArithmetic = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 1 + 1"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 2

testReturnTable :: Assertion
testReturnTable = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return {foo = 'bar', count = 42}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["foo" .= "bar", "count" .= (42 :: Int)]

testReturnArray :: Assertion
testReturnArray = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return {1, 2, 3}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.Array (Aeson.fromList [Aeson.Number 1, Aeson.Number 2, Aeson.Number 3])

testReturnString :: Assertion
testReturnString = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 'hello world'"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello world"

testReturnBoolean :: Assertion
testReturnBoolean = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return true"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testReturnNil :: Assertion
testReturnNil = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return nil"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Null

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Sandbox Security Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

sandboxSecurityTests :: TestTree
sandboxSecurityTests =
    testGroup
        "Sandbox Security"
        [ testCase "Sandbox blocks os.execute" testSandboxBlocksOsExecute
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.popen" testSandboxBlocksIoPopen
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks dofile" testSandboxBlocksDofile
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks loadfile" testSandboxBlocksLoadfile
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.remove" testSandboxBlocksOsRemove
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.rename" testSandboxBlocksOsRename
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.exit" testSandboxBlocksOsExit
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.tmpfile" testSandboxBlocksIoTmpfile
        ]

testSandboxBlocksOsExecute :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsExecute = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.execute('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksIoPopen :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksIoPopen = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "io.popen('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksDofile :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksDofile = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "dofile('/etc/passwd')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksLoadfile :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksLoadfile = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "loadfile('/etc/passwd')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksOsRemove :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsRemove = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.remove('/tmp/test')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksOsRename :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsRename = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.rename('/tmp/a', '/tmp/b')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksOsExit :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsExit = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.exit(0)"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksIoTmpfile :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksIoTmpfile = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "io.tmpfile()"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- JSON Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleJsonTests :: TestTree
moduleJsonTests =
    testGroup
        "JSON Module"
        [ testCase "JSON encode" testJsonEncode
        , testCase "JSON decode" testJsonDecode
        , testCase "JSON roundtrip" testJsonRoundtrip
        , testCase "JSON decode error handling" testJsonDecodeError
        ]

testJsonEncode :: Assertion
testJsonEncode = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello'}"
                , "return json.encode(data)"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- The result should be a JSON string
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain x:1" $ "1" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

testJsonDecode :: Assertion
testJsonDecode = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local obj = json.decode('\"hello\"')"
                , "return obj"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello"

testJsonRoundtrip :: Assertion
testJsonRoundtrip = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello', z = true}"
                , "local encoded = json.encode(data)"
                , "local decoded = json.decode(encoded)"
                , "return decoded"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["x" .= (1 :: Int), "y" .= "hello", "z" .= True]

testJsonDecodeError :: Assertion
testJsonDecodeError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local ok, err = pcall(function() json.decode('invalid json') end)"
                , "return {ok = ok, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "ok" obj of
                        Just (Aeson.Bool b) -> b @?= False
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected ok=false"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Text Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleTextTests :: TestTree
moduleTextTests =
    testGroup
        "Text Module"
        [ testCase "text.split" testTextSplit
        , testCase "text.find" testTextFind
        , testCase "text.trim" testTextTrim
        , testCase "text.startswith" testTextStartswith
        , testCase "text.endswith" testTextEndswith
        , testCase "text.lower" testTextLower
        , testCase "text.upper" testTextUpper
        , testCase "text.len" testTextLen
        , testCase "text.gsub" testTextGsub
        ]

testTextSplit :: Assertion
testTextSplit = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "local parts = text.split('a,b,c', ',')"
                , "return {count = #parts, first = parts[1]}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["count" .= (3 :: Int), "first" .= "a"]

testTextFind :: Assertion
testTextFind = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "local start, finish = text.find('hello world', 'world')"
                , "return {startPos = start, endPos = finish}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["startPos" .= (7 :: Int), "endPos" .= (11 :: Int)]

testTextTrim :: Assertion
testTextTrim = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return text.trim('  hello world  ')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello world"

testTextStartswith :: Assertion
testTextStartswith = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return {result = text.startswith('hello world', 'hello')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.object ["result" .= True]

testTextEndswith :: Assertion
testTextEndswith = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return {result = text.endswith('hello world', 'world')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.object ["result" .= True]

testTextLower :: Assertion
testTextLower = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return text.lower('HELLO WORLD')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello world"

testTextUpper :: Assertion
testTextUpper = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return text.upper('hello world')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "HELLO WORLD"

testTextLen :: Assertion
testTextLen = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return text.len('hello')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 5

testTextGsub :: Assertion
testTextGsub = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "local result, count = text.gsub('hello world', 'world', 'lua')"
                , "return {text = result, replacements = count}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["text" .= "hello lua", "replacements" .= (1 :: Int)]

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Time Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleTimeTests :: TestTree
moduleTimeTests =
    testGroup
        "Time Module"
        [ testCase "time.now returns timestamp" testTimeNow
        , testCase "time.sleep works" testTimeSleep
        , testCase "time.diff calculates difference" testTimeDiff
        , testCase "time.format formats timestamp" testTimeFormat
        ]

testTimeNow :: Assertion
testTimeNow = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local now = time.now()"
                , "return now"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Number n ->
                    -- Should be a reasonable timestamp (after 2020)
                    assertBool "Timestamp should be positive" (n > 0)
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected number (timestamp)"

testTimeSleep :: Assertion
testTimeSleep = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    -- Sleep for a very short time (0.01 seconds)
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local ok = time.sleep(0.01)"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testTimeDiff :: Assertion
testTimeDiff = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local diff = time.diff(100, 50)"
                , "return diff"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 50

testTimeFormat :: Assertion
testTimeFormat = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "-- Format Unix epoch 0 (1970-01-01)"
                , "local formatted = time.format(0, '%Y-%m-%d')"
                , "return formatted"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain 1970" $ "1970" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Filesystem Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleFsTests :: TestTree
moduleFsTests =
    testGroup
        "Filesystem Module"
        [ testCase "fs.read reads file" testFsRead
        , testCase "fs.write writes file" testFsWrite
        , testCase "fs.exists checks existence" testFsExists
        , testCase "fs.isdir checks directory" testFsIsDir
        , testCase "fs.isfile checks file" testFsIsFile
        , testCase "fs.mkdir creates directory" testFsMkdir
        , testCase "fs.list lists directory" testFsList
        , testCase "fs sandbox blocks unauthorized path" testFsSandbox
        ]

testFsRead :: Assertion
testFsRead = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "test.txt") "hello world"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return fs.read('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "test.txt") <> "')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain hello" $ "hello" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

testFsWrite :: Assertion
testFsWrite = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local ok, err = fs.write('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "output.txt") <> "', 'test content')"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testFsExists :: Assertion
testFsExists = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "exists.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {exists = fs.exists('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "exists.txt") <> "'), missing = fs.exists('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "missing.txt") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["exists" .= True, "missing" .= False]

testFsIsDir :: Assertion
testFsIsDir = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    createDirectoryIfMissing True (testDir </> "subdir")
    writeFile (testDir </> "file.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {dir = fs.isdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "subdir") <> "'), file = fs.isdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "file.txt") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["dir" .= True, "file" .= False]

testFsIsFile :: Assertion
testFsIsFile = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    createDirectoryIfMissing True (testDir </> "subdir")
    writeFile (testDir </> "file.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {file = fs.isfile('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "file.txt") <> "'), dir = fs.isfile('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "subdir") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["file" .= True, "dir" .= False]

testFsMkdir :: Assertion
testFsMkdir = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local ok = fs.mkdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "newdir") <> "')"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testFsList :: Assertion
testFsList = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "file1.txt") ""
    writeFile (testDir </> "file2.txt") ""
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local entries, err = fs.list('" <> Text.pack testDir <> "')"
                , "return #entries"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- Should have at least 2 files
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Number n -> assertBool "Should have at least 2 files" (n >= 2)
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected number"

testFsSandbox :: Assertion
testFsSandbox = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    -- Try to access a path outside the sandbox
    let unauthorizedPath = "/etc/passwd"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local content, err = fs.read('" <> Text.pack unauthorizedPath <> "')"
                , "return {content = content, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- Should return nil content with error
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "content" obj of
                        Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: content is nil
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil content"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Resource Limit Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

resourceLimitTests :: TestTree
resourceLimitTests =
    testGroup
        "Resource Limits"
        [ testCase "Timeout enforcement" testTimeout
        -- Note: Memory limit is difficult to test reliably in CI
        -- Skipping for now to avoid flakiness
        ]

testTimeout :: Assertion
testTimeout = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 1}
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "while true do end"
            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer box
            case result of
                Left (TimeoutError _) -> pure () -- Expected
                Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
                Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have timed out"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Error Handling Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

errorHandlingTests :: TestTree
errorHandlingTests =
    testGroup
        "Error Handling"
        [ testCase "Syntax error handling" testSyntaxError
        , testCase "Runtime error handling" testRuntimeError
        , testCase "Error handling with pcall" testPcallError
        ]

testSyntaxError :: Assertion
testSyntaxError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 1 +"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention syntax" $
                "syntax" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "expected" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with syntax error"

testRuntimeError :: Assertion
testRuntimeError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return nonexistent_variable"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with runtime error"

testPcallError :: Assertion
testPcallError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local ok, err = pcall(function() return nonexistent end)"
                , "return {success = ok, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "success" obj of
                        Just (Aeson.Bool b) -> b @?= False
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected success=false"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Portal Integration Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

portalIntegrationTests :: TestTree
portalIntegrationTests =
    testGroup
        "Portal Integration"
        [ testCase "Tool call with mock portal" testPortalToolCall
        , testCase "Tool call without portal fails gracefully" testNoPortal
        , testCase "Tool whitelist enforcement" testToolWhitelist
        ]

testPortalToolCall :: Assertion
testPortalToolCall = do
    -- Create a mock portal that echoes back the call
    let mockPortal call =
            pure $
                ToolResult
                    { resultData =
                        Aeson.object
                            [ "tool" .= callToolName call
                            , "args" .= callArgs call
                            ]
                    , resultDuration = 0.1
                    , resultTraceId = "test-trace-123"
                    }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["test_tool"]}

    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <-
                LuaToolbox.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    ( Text.unlines
                        [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                        , "local result = tools.call('test_tool', {x = 1})"
                        , "return result"
                        ]
                    )
                    (Just mockPortal)
                    ["test_tool"]
                    nullTracerTools

            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer box

            case result of
                Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
                Right execResult -> do
                    case execResult.resultValue of
                        Aeson.Object resultObj -> do
                            case KeyMap.lookup "data" resultObj of
                                Just (Aeson.Object dataObj) -> do
                                    case KeyMap.lookup "tool" dataObj of
                                        Just (Aeson.String t) -> t @?= "test_tool"
                                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected tool name in data"
                                _ -> assertFailure "Expected data object"
                        _ -> assertFailure $ "Unexpected result: " ++ show execResult.resultValue

testNoPortal :: Assertion
testNoPortal = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    -- Try to call a tool without a portal
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                , "local result, err = tools.call('bash', {command = 'echo hello'})"
                , "return {result = result, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "result" obj of
                        Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: result is nil
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil result"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

testToolWhitelist :: Assertion
testToolWhitelist = do
    -- Create a mock portal that should not be called for blocked tool
    let mockPortal _ =
            pure $
                ToolResult
                    { resultData = Aeson.object ["result" .= "success"]
                    , resultDuration = 0.1
                    , resultTraceId = "test-trace"
                    }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["allowed_tool"]}

    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <-
                LuaToolbox.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    ( Text.unlines
                        [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                        , "local result, err = tools.call('blocked_tool', {})"
                        , "return {result = result, error = err}"
                        ]
                    )
                    (Just mockPortal)
                    ["allowed_tool"]
                    nullTracerTools

            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer box

            case result of
                Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
                Right execResult ->
                    case execResult.resultValue of
                        Aeson.Object obj -> do
                            case KeyMap.lookup "result" obj of
                                Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: result is nil
                                _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil result for blocked tool"
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Helper Functions
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

testLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription
testLuaToolbox =
    LuaToolboxDescription
        { luaToolboxName = "test"
        , luaToolboxDescription = "Test toolbox"
        , luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 64
        , luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 10
        , luaToolboxAllowedTools = []
        , luaToolboxAllowedPaths = []
        , luaToolboxAllowedHosts = []
        }

nullTracer :: Tracer IO LuaToolbox.Trace
nullTracer = Tracer $ const $ pure ()

nullTracerTools :: Tracer IO System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Tools.Trace
nullTracerTools = Tracer $ const $ pure ()

-- | Run an action with a test toolbox initialized
withTestToolbox :: (LuaToolbox.Toolbox -> IO ()) -> IO ()
withTestToolbox action = do
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer testLuaToolbox
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize toolbox: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            bracket
                (pure box)
                (\b -> LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer b)
                action

-- | Run an action with a test toolbox that has filesystem access
withTestToolboxFs :: (LuaToolbox.Toolbox -> FilePath -> IO ()) -> IO ()
withTestToolboxFs action = withTempSandbox $ \testDir -> do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedPaths = [testDir]}
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize toolbox: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            bracket
                (pure (box, testDir))
                (\(b, _) -> LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer b)
                (\(b, d) -> action b d)

-- | Create a temporary sandbox directory for testing
withTempSandbox :: (FilePath -> IO a) -> IO a
withTempSandbox action = do
    tmpDir <- createTempDirectory "/tmp" "luatoolbox-test-"
    bracket
        (pure tmpDir)
        removeDirectoryRecursive
        action
"}

{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}

-- | Comprehensive unit tests for LuaToolbox.
--
-- These tests verify:
-- * Basic Lua execution (arithmetic, tables)
-- * Sandbox security (os.execute, io.popen blocked)
-- * Standard library modules (json, text, time)
-- * Resource limits (memory, timeout)
-- * Filesystem sandboxing
-- * HTTP sandboxing
-- * Error handling
module LuaToolboxTests where

import Control.Exception (bracket)
import Data.Aeson ((.=))
import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import System.Directory (createDirectoryIfMissing, removeDirectoryRecursive)
import System.FilePath ((</>))
import System.IO (writeFile)
import System.IO.Temp (createTempDirectory)
import Test.Tasty
import Test.Tasty.HUnit

import Prod.Tracer (Tracer (..))

import System.Agents.Base (LuaToolboxDescription (..))
import System.Agents.Tools.Context (ToolCall (..), ToolPortal, ToolResult (..))
import System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox as LuaToolbox

luaToolboxTests :: TestTree
luaToolboxTests =
    testGroup
        "LuaToolbox Tests"
        [ basicExecutionTests
        , sandboxSecurityTests
        , moduleJsonTests
        , moduleTextTests
        , moduleTimeTests
        , moduleFsTests
        , resourceLimitTests
        , errorHandlingTests
        , portalIntegrationTests
        ]

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Basic Execution Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

basicExecutionTests :: TestTree
basicExecutionTests =
    testGroup
        "Basic Execution"
        [ testCase "Simple arithmetic" testSimpleArithmetic
        , testCase "Return table" testReturnTable
        , testCase "Return array" testReturnArray
        , testCase "Return string" testReturnString
        , testCase "Return boolean" testReturnBoolean
        , testCase "Return nil" testReturnNil
        ]

testSimpleArithmetic :: Assertion
testSimpleArithmetic = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 1 + 1"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 2

testReturnTable :: Assertion
testReturnTable = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return {foo = 'bar', count = 42}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["foo" .= "bar", "count" .= (42 :: Int)]

testReturnArray :: Assertion
testReturnArray = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return {1, 2, 3}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.Array (Aeson.fromList [Aeson.Number 1, Aeson.Number 2, Aeson.Number 3])

testReturnString :: Assertion
testReturnString = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 'hello world'"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello world"

testReturnBoolean :: Assertion
testReturnBoolean = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return true"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testReturnNil :: Assertion
testReturnNil = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return nil"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Null

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Sandbox Security Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

sandboxSecurityTests :: TestTree
sandboxSecurityTests =
    testGroup
        "Sandbox Security"
        [ testCase "Sandbox blocks os.execute" testSandboxBlocksOsExecute
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.popen" testSandboxBlocksIoPopen
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks dofile" testSandboxBlocksDofile
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks loadfile" testSandboxBlocksLoadfile
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.remove" testSandboxBlocksOsRemove
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.rename" testSandboxBlocksOsRename
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.exit" testSandboxBlocksOsExit
        , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.tmpfile" testSandboxBlocksIoTmpfile
        ]

testSandboxBlocksOsExecute :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsExecute = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.execute('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksIoPopen :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksIoPopen = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "io.popen('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksDofile :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksDofile = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "dofile('/etc/passwd')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksLoadfile :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksLoadfile = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "loadfile('/etc/passwd')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksOsRemove :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsRemove = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.remove('/tmp/test')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksOsRename :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsRename = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.rename('/tmp/a', '/tmp/b')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksOsExit :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsExit = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "os.exit(0)"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksIoTmpfile :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksIoTmpfile = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "io.tmpfile()"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- JSON Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleJsonTests :: TestTree
moduleJsonTests =
    testGroup
        "JSON Module"
        [ testCase "JSON encode" testJsonEncode
        , testCase "JSON decode" testJsonDecode
        , testCase "JSON roundtrip" testJsonRoundtrip
        , testCase "JSON decode error handling" testJsonDecodeError
        ]

testJsonEncode :: Assertion
testJsonEncode = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello'}"
                , "return json.encode(data)"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- The result should be a JSON string
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain x:1" $ "1" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

testJsonDecode :: Assertion
testJsonDecode = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local obj = json.decode('\"hello\"')"
                , "return obj"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello"

testJsonRoundtrip :: Assertion
testJsonRoundtrip = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello', z = true}"
                , "local encoded = json.encode(data)"
                , "local decoded = json.decode(encoded)"
                , "return decoded"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["x" .= (1 :: Int), "y" .= "hello", "z" .= True]

testJsonDecodeError :: Assertion
testJsonDecodeError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local json = require('json')"
                , "local ok, err = pcall(function() json.decode('invalid json') end)"
                , "return {ok = ok, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "ok" obj of
                        Just (Aeson.Bool b) -> b @?= False
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected ok=false"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Text Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleTextTests :: TestTree
moduleTextTests =
    testGroup
        "Text Module"
        [ testCase "text.split" testTextSplit
        , testCase "text.find" testTextFind
        , testCase "text.trim" testTextTrim
        , testCase "text.startswith" testTextStartswith
        , testCase "text.endswith" testTextEndswith
        , testCase "text.lower" testTextLower
        , testCase "text.upper" testTextUpper
        , testCase "text.len" testTextLen
        , testCase "text.gsub" testTextGsub
        ]

testTextSplit :: Assertion
testTextSplit = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "local parts = text.split('a,b,c', ',')"
                , "return {count = #parts, first = parts[1]}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["count" .= (3 :: Int), "first" .= "a"]

testTextFind :: Assertion
testTextFind = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "local start, finish = text.find('hello world', 'world')"
                , "return {startPos = start, endPos = finish}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["startPos" .= (7 :: Int), "endPos" .= (11 :: Int)]

testTextTrim :: Assertion
testTextTrim = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return text.trim('  hello world  ')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello world"

testTextStartswith :: Assertion
testTextStartswith = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return {result = text.startswith('hello world', 'hello')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.object ["result" .= True]

testTextEndswith :: Assertion
testTextEndswith = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return {result = text.endswith('hello world', 'world')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.object ["result" .= True]

testTextLower :: Assertion
testTextLower = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return text.lower('HELLO WORLD')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "hello world"

testTextUpper :: Assertion
testTextUpper = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return text.upper('hello world')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.String "HELLO WORLD"

testTextLen :: Assertion
testTextLen = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "return text.len('hello')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 5

testTextGsub :: Assertion
testTextGsub = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local text = require('text')"
                , "local result, count = text.gsub('hello world', 'world', 'lua')"
                , "return {text = result, replacements = count}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["text" .= "hello lua", "replacements" .= (1 :: Int)]

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Time Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleTimeTests :: TestTree
moduleTimeTests =
    testGroup
        "Time Module"
        [ testCase "time.now returns timestamp" testTimeNow
        , testCase "time.sleep works" testTimeSleep
        , testCase "time.diff calculates difference" testTimeDiff
        , testCase "time.format formats timestamp" testTimeFormat
        ]

testTimeNow :: Assertion
testTimeNow = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local now = time.now()"
                , "return now"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Number n ->
                    -- Should be a reasonable timestamp (after 2020)
                    assertBool "Timestamp should be positive" (n > 0)
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected number (timestamp)"

testTimeSleep :: Assertion
testTimeSleep = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    -- Sleep for a very short time (0.01 seconds)
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local ok = time.sleep(0.01)"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testTimeDiff :: Assertion
testTimeDiff = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "local diff = time.diff(100, 50)"
                , "return diff"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 50

testTimeFormat :: Assertion
testTimeFormat = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local time = require('time')"
                , "-- Format Unix epoch 0 (1970-01-01)"
                , "local formatted = time.format(0, '%Y-%m-%d')"
                , "return formatted"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain 1970" $ "1970" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Filesystem Module Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

moduleFsTests :: TestTree
moduleFsTests =
    testGroup
        "Filesystem Module"
        [ testCase "fs.read reads file" testFsRead
        , testCase "fs.write writes file" testFsWrite
        , testCase "fs.exists checks existence" testFsExists
        , testCase "fs.isdir checks directory" testFsIsDir
        , testCase "fs.isfile checks file" testFsIsFile
        , testCase "fs.mkdir creates directory" testFsMkdir
        , testCase "fs.list lists directory" testFsList
        , testCase "fs sandbox blocks unauthorized path" testFsSandbox
        ]

testFsRead :: Assertion
testFsRead = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "test.txt") "hello world"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return fs.read('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "test.txt") <> "')"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.String s ->
                    assertBool "Should contain hello" $ "hello" `Text.isInfixOf` s
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected string result"

testFsWrite :: Assertion
testFsWrite = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local ok, err = fs.write('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "output.txt") <> "', 'test content')"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testFsExists :: Assertion
testFsExists = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "exists.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {exists = fs.exists('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "exists.txt") <> "'), missing = fs.exists('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "missing.txt") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["exists" .= True, "missing" .= False]

testFsIsDir :: Assertion
testFsIsDir = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    createDirectoryIfMissing True (testDir </> "subdir")
    writeFile (testDir </> "file.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {dir = fs.isdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "subdir") <> "'), file = fs.isdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "file.txt") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["dir" .= True, "file" .= False]

testFsIsFile :: Assertion
testFsIsFile = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    createDirectoryIfMissing True (testDir </> "subdir")
    writeFile (testDir </> "file.txt") "content"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "return {file = fs.isfile('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "file.txt") <> "'), dir = fs.isfile('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "subdir") <> "')}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue
                @?= Aeson.object ["file" .= True, "dir" .= False]

testFsMkdir :: Assertion
testFsMkdir = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local ok = fs.mkdir('" <> Text.pack (testDir </> "newdir") <> "')"
                , "return ok"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Bool True

testFsList :: Assertion
testFsList = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    writeFile (testDir </> "file1.txt") ""
    writeFile (testDir </> "file2.txt") ""
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local entries, err = fs.list('" <> Text.pack testDir <> "')"
                , "return #entries"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- Should have at least 2 files
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Number n -> assertBool "Should have at least 2 files" (n >= 2)
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected number"

testFsSandbox :: Assertion
testFsSandbox = withTestToolboxFs $ \box testDir -> do
    -- Try to access a path outside the sandbox
    let unauthorizedPath = "/etc/passwd"
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local fs = require('fs')"
                , "local content, err = fs.read('" <> Text.pack unauthorizedPath <> "')"
                , "return {content = content, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            -- Should return nil content with error
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "content" obj of
                        Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: content is nil
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil content"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Resource Limit Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

resourceLimitTests :: TestTree
resourceLimitTests =
    testGroup
        "Resource Limits"
        [ testCase "Timeout enforcement" testTimeout
        -- Note: Memory limit is difficult to test reliably in CI
        -- Skipping for now to avoid flakiness
        ]

testTimeout :: Assertion
testTimeout = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 1}
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "while true do end"
            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer box
            case result of
                Left (TimeoutError _) -> pure () -- Expected
                Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
                Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have timed out"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Error Handling Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

errorHandlingTests :: TestTree
errorHandlingTests =
    testGroup
        "Error Handling"
        [ testCase "Syntax error handling" testSyntaxError
        , testCase "Runtime error handling" testRuntimeError
        , testCase "Error handling with pcall" testPcallError
        ]

testSyntaxError :: Assertion
testSyntaxError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return 1 +"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention syntax" $
                "syntax" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "expected" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with syntax error"

testRuntimeError :: Assertion
testRuntimeError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <- LuaToolbox.executeScript box "return nonexistent_variable"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil or attempt" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left _ -> pure () -- Any error is acceptable
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with runtime error"

testPcallError :: Assertion
testPcallError = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local ok, err = pcall(function() return nonexistent end)"
                , "return {success = ok, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "success" obj of
                        Just (Aeson.Bool b) -> b @?= False
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected success=false"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Portal Integration Tests
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

portalIntegrationTests :: TestTree
portalIntegrationTests =
    testGroup
        "Portal Integration"
        [ testCase "Tool call with mock portal" testPortalToolCall
        , testCase "Tool call without portal fails gracefully" testNoPortal
        , testCase "Tool whitelist enforcement" testToolWhitelist
        ]

testPortalToolCall :: Assertion
testPortalToolCall = do
    -- Create a mock portal that echoes back the call
    let mockPortal call =
            pure $
                ToolResult
                    { resultData =
                        Aeson.object
                            [ "tool" .= callToolName call
                            , "args" .= callArgs call
                            ]
                    , resultDuration = 0.1
                    , resultTraceId = "test-trace-123"
                    }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["test_tool"]}

    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <-
                LuaToolbox.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    ( Text.unlines
                        [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                        , "local result = tools.call('test_tool', {x = 1})"
                        , "return result"
                        ]
                    )
                    (Just mockPortal)
                    ["test_tool"]
                    nullTracerTools

            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer box

            case result of
                Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
                Right execResult -> do
                    case execResult.resultValue of
                        Aeson.Object resultObj -> do
                            case KeyMap.lookup "data" resultObj of
                                Just (Aeson.Object dataObj) -> do
                                    case KeyMap.lookup "tool" dataObj of
                                        Just (Aeson.String t) -> t @?= "test_tool"
                                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected tool name in data"
                                _ -> assertFailure "Expected data object"
                        _ -> assertFailure $ "Unexpected result: " ++ show execResult.resultValue

testNoPortal :: Assertion
testNoPortal = withTestToolbox $ \box -> do
    -- Try to call a tool without a portal
    result <-
        LuaToolbox.executeScript box $
            Text.unlines
                [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                , "local result, err = tools.call('bash', {command = 'echo hello'})"
                , "return {result = result, error = err}"
                ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            case execResult.resultValue of
                Aeson.Object obj -> do
                    case KeyMap.lookup "result" obj of
                        Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: result is nil
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil result"
                _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

testToolWhitelist :: Assertion
testToolWhitelist = do
    -- Create a mock portal that should not be called for blocked tool
    let mockPortal _ =
            pure $
                ToolResult
                    { resultData = Aeson.object ["result" .= "success"]
                    , resultDuration = 0.1
                    , resultTraceId = "test-trace"
                    }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["allowed_tool"]}

    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            result <-
                LuaToolbox.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    ( Text.unlines
                        [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                        , "local result, err = tools.call('blocked_tool', {})"
                        , "return {result = result, error = err}"
                        ]
                    )
                    (Just mockPortal)
                    ["allowed_tool"]
                    nullTracerTools

            LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer box

            case result of
                Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
                Right execResult ->
                    case execResult.resultValue of
                        Aeson.Object obj -> do
                            case KeyMap.lookup "result" obj of
                                Just Aeson.Null -> pure () -- Expected: result is nil
                                _ -> assertFailure "Expected nil result for blocked tool"
                        _ -> assertFailure "Expected object result"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Helper Functions
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

testLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription
testLuaToolbox =
    LuaToolboxDescription
        { luaToolboxName = "test"
        , luaToolboxDescription = "Test toolbox"
        , luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 64
        , luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 10
        , luaToolboxAllowedTools = []
        , luaToolboxAllowedPaths = []
        , luaToolboxAllowedHosts = []
        }

nullTracer :: Tracer IO LuaToolbox.Trace
nullTracer = Tracer $ const $ pure ()

nullTracerTools :: Tracer IO System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Tools.Trace
nullTracerTools = Tracer $ const $ pure ()

-- | Run an action with a test toolbox initialized
withTestToolbox :: (LuaToolbox.Toolbox -> IO ()) -> IO ()
withTestToolbox action = do
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer testLuaToolbox
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize toolbox: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            bracket
                (pure box)
                (\b -> LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer b)
                action

-- | Run an action with a test toolbox that has filesystem access
withTestToolboxFs :: (LuaToolbox.Toolbox -> FilePath -> IO ()) -> IO ()
withTestToolboxFs action = withTempSandbox $ \testDir -> do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox{luaToolboxAllowedPaths = [testDir]}
    initResult <- LuaToolbox.initializeToolbox nullTracer desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Failed to initialize toolbox: " ++ err
        Right box -> do
            bracket
                (pure (box, testDir))
                (\(b, _) -> LuaToolbox.closeToolbox nullTracer b)
                (\(b, d) -> action b d)

-- | Create a temporary sandbox directory for testing
withTempSandbox :: (FilePath -> IO a) -> IO a
withTempSandbox action = do
    tmpDir <- createTempDirectory "/tmp" "luatoolbox-test-"
    bracket
        (pure tmpDir)
        removeDirectoryRecursive
        action
"}

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

-- | JSON module for LuaToolbox.
module System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox.Modules.Json (
    registerJsonModule,
) where

import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Text as AesonText
import Data.Scientific (Scientific, floatingOrInteger, fromFloatDigits)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Lazy as LazyText
import qualified Data.Vector as Vector
import qualified HsLua as Lua
import qualified HsLua.Marshalling as Lua

import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey
import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap

-- | Register the json module in the Lua state.
registerJsonModule :: Lua.State -> IO ()
registerJsonModule lstate = Lua.runWith lstate $ do
    Lua.newtable

    Lua.pushName "encode"
    Lua.pushHaskellFunction luaEncode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    Lua.pushName "decode"
    Lua.pushHaskellFunction luaDecode
    Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)

    Lua.setglobal (Lua.Name "json")

-- | Encode a Lua value to JSON string.
luaEncode :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception Lua.NumResults
luaEncode = do
    jsonVal <- luaToAesonValue
    let txt = LazyText.toStrict $ AesonText.encodeToLazyText jsonVal
    Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 txt)
    pure 1

-- | Decode a JSON string to a Lua value.
luaDecode :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception Lua.NumResults
luaDecode = do
    str <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
    Lua.pop 1
    case Aeson.eitherDecodeStrict' str of
        Left err -> do
            Lua.pushnil
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack err)
            pure 2
        Right val -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            pure 1

-- | Convert Lua value at top of stack to Aeson Value.
luaToAesonValue :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception Aeson.Value
luaToAesonValue = do
    ltype <- Lua.ltype (Lua.nthTop 1)
    case ltype of
        Lua.TypeNil -> do
            Lua.pop 1
            pure Aeson.Null
        Lua.TypeBoolean -> do
            b <- Lua.toboolean (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.Bool b
        Lua.TypeNumber -> do
            mNum <- Lua.tonumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            case mNum of
                Nothing -> pure Aeson.Null
                Just n -> pure $ Aeson.Number $ fromFloatDigits n
        Lua.TypeString -> do
            s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
            Lua.pop 1
            pure $ Aeson.String (Text.decodeUtf8 s)
        Lua.TypeTable -> do
            convertTable
        _ -> do
            str <- luaValueToString
            pure $ Aeson.String str
  where
    luaValueToString :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception Text
    luaValueToString = do
        Lua.pushglobaltable
        Lua.pushName (Lua.Name "tostring")
        _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
        Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
        _ <- Lua.pcall 1 1 Nothing
        s <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 1)
        Lua.pop 1
        pure $ Text.decodeUtf8 s

convertTable :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception Aeson.Value
convertTable = do
    isArr <- isArray
    if isArr
        then convertArray
        else convertObject

isArray :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception Bool
isArray = do
    Lua.pushnil
    isSequential 1
  where
    isSequential expectedIdx = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then pure True
            else do
                isNum <- Lua.isnumber (Lua.nthTop 1)
                if isNum
                    then do
                        mIdx <- Lua.tointeger (Lua.nthTop 1)
                        Lua.pop 2
                        case mIdx of
                            Just idx
                                | idx == fromIntegral expectedIdx ->
                                    isSequential (expectedIdx + 1)
                            _ -> pure False
                    else do
                        Lua.pop 2
                        pure False

convertArray :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception Aeson.Value
convertArray = do
    vals <- collectArrayValues
    pure $ Aeson.Array (Vector.fromList vals)

collectArrayValues :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception [Aeson.Value]
collectArrayValues = do
    len' <- Lua.rawlen (Lua.nthTop 1)
    vals <-
        mapM
            ( \i -> do
                Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
                _ <- Lua.gettable (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                pure val
            )
            [1 .. fromIntegral len']
    Lua.pop 1
    pure vals

convertObject :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception Aeson.Value
convertObject = do
    pairs <- collectObjectPairs
    pure $ Aeson.Object (KeyMap.fromList pairs)

collectObjectPairs :: Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception [(AesonKey.Key, Aeson.Value)]
collectObjectPairs = do
    Lua.pushnil
    go []
  where
    go acc = do
        hasNext <- Lua.next (Lua.nthTop 2)
        if not hasNext
            then do
                Lua.pop 1
                pure acc
            else do
                key <- Lua.tostring' (Lua.nthTop 2)
                val <- luaToAesonValue
                Lua.pop 1
                go ((AesonKey.fromText (Text.decodeUtf8 key), val) : acc)

aesonToLuaValue :: Aeson.Value -> Lua.LuaE Lua.Exception ()
aesonToLuaValue Aeson.Null = Lua.pushnil
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Bool b) = Lua.pushboolean b
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Number n) =
    case floatingOrInteger n of
        Left d -> Lua.pushnumber d
        Right i -> Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral i)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.String t) = Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 t)
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Array arr) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_
        ( \(i, val) -> do
            aesonToLuaValue val
            Lua.pushinteger (fromIntegral (i + 1))
            Lua.insert (Lua.nthTop 2)
            Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        )
        (zip [0 ..] (Vector.toList arr))
aesonToLuaValue (Aeson.Object obj) = do
    Lua.newtable
    mapM_
        ( \(k, val) -> do
            Lua.pushstring (Text.encodeUtf8 $ AesonKey.toText k)
            aesonToLuaValue val
            Lua.settable (Lua.nthTop 3)
        )
        (KeyMap.toList obj)
